creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.
contentSnippet: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

title: (No AI) Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom

title: (No AI) Anthropic is launching Claude Cowork on mobile and web
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![A phone and laptop side by side running Claude Cowork](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Cowork-Web-Mobile-Press-No-Logo-1920x1080-1.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Starting Tuesday, Anthropic's [Claude Cowork AI platform](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/860730/anthropic-cowork-feature-ai-agents-claude-code) will be [available on mobile and web](https://claude.com/blog/cowork-web-mobile/) for the first time. The expanded access is rolling out first to Max subscribers and coming to Claude users on other plans "in the coming weeks."

Claude Cowork was previously only accessible through the Claude desktop app for macOS and Windows, but now users on iOS and Android can also use it. However, Anthropic says the "full experience" for Cowork will still be on the desktop app, including features like local file access.

Cowork sessions will also now run in the cloud by default, so you can continue them across different devices or run Cowork ta …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/961978/anthropic-claude-cowork-mobile-web)

Starting Tuesday, Anthropic's Claude Cowork AI platform will be available on mobile and web for the first time. The expanded access is rolling out first to Max subscribers and coming to Claude users on other plans "in the coming weeks." 
Claude Cowork was previously only accessible through the Claude desktop app for macOS and Windows, but now users on iOS and Android can also use it. However, Anthropic says the "full experience" for Cowork will still be on the desktop app, including features like local file access. 
Cowork sessions will also now run in the cloud by default, so you can continue them across different devices or run Cowork ta …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The expanded access is rolling out first to Max subscribers and coming to Claude users on other plans "in the coming weeks." Claude Cowork was previously only accessible through the Claude desktop app for macOS and Windows, […]

title: (No AI) Solos debuts an even lighter version of its camera-less smart glasses
author: Andrew Liszewski
content:
![Solos’ AirGo A6 smart glasses in a clear shiny honey design.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/solos1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The AirGo A6 are available in multiple designs including several transparent color options. | Image: Solos

Solos announced a new version of its AirGo smart glasses, one that forgoes cameras for a sleeker design and an AI assistant that relies on voice interactions. Last year's AirGo A5 weighed 36 to 40 grams depending on the frame style, but the new AirGo A6 weigh around 19 grams. Part of the weight savings comes from thinner temple arms housing speakers, batteries, and other electronics. For comparison, the [new Meta Glasses announced last month](https://www.theverge.com/tech/954052/meta-glasses-hands-on-kylie-jenner-smart-glasses-price-battery-privacy) weigh around 54 to nearly 60 grams depending on the style.

Pricing and availability for the new AirGo A6 hasn't been finalized yet, but the smart glasses will support "full prescription lens compatibilit …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/961711/solos-airgo-a6-smart-glasses-ai-assistant-privacy)

title: (No AI) How ChatGPT adoption has expanded
content: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.
contentSnippet: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.

title: (No AI) Core dump epidemiology: fixing an 18-year-old bug
content: OpenAI engineers used large-scale core dump analysis to debug rare infrastructure crashes, uncovering both a hardware fault and a long-standing software bug.
contentSnippet: OpenAI engineers used large-scale core dump analysis to debug rare infrastructure crashes, uncovering both a hardware fault and a long-standing software bug.

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) Shut Those Laptops! Anthropic Puts Its Claude Cowork Agent on Your Phone
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: Claude Cowork now keeps working on tasks even after you close your laptop. It’s part of a larger push toward smartphone-controlled agents.
contentSnippet: Claude Cowork now keeps working on tasks even after you close your laptop. It’s part of a larger push toward smartphone-controlled agents.

title: (No AI) These New Smart Glasses From Solos Come With a Privacy Shield for the Cameras
